facts.text: The Academy Awards, commonly called the Oscars, are presented annually by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences.
The first Academy Awards ceremony took place on May 16, 1929, at the Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel.
The first Oscars ceremony lasted only about 15 minutes.
The winners of the very first Academy Awards were announced in advance, unlike the sealed-envelope suspense used today.
The Oscar statuette stands about 13.5 inches tall and weighs roughly 8.5 pounds.
The award is gold-plated and depicts a knight holding a crusader's sword while standing on a film reel.
Best Picture is the ceremony's most prestigious prize, awarded to the producers of a film.
Walt Disney won more competitive Oscars than any other individual in history.
Katharine Hepburn holds the acting record with four Academy Awards.
Meryl Streep holds the record for the most acting nominations.
Ben-Hur, Titanic, and The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King each won 11 Oscars, tying the all-time record.
The Return of the King won every category in which it was nominated, completing the Lord of the Rings trilogy with a historic sweep.
Only a few sequels have ever won Best Picture, including The Godfather Part II and The Return of the King.
The Academy now includes members from many branches of filmmaking, including actors, directors, writers, editors, composers, and designers.
Foreign-language and international cinema became more visible at the Oscars over time, especially with the International Feature Film category.
Parasite made history at the 92nd Academy Awards by becoming the first non-English-language film to win Best Picture.
The Oscars were one of the earliest award shows to become a major live television event.
Red carpet fashion at the Academy Awards became its own global media spectacle.
The nickname 'Oscar' has murky origins, but it became official shorthand for the award decades after the ceremony began.
An Academy Award can dramatically boost a film's box office, prestige, and long-term cultural reputation.
The Best Animated Feature category was introduced in 2001, giving animated films a dedicated top-level Oscar race.
The Best Picture lineup expanded from five nominees to as many as ten in order to recognize a broader range of films.
The Academy periodically changes its rules and eligibility requirements in response to industry shifts, new technology, and inclusion efforts.
Acceptance speeches at the Oscars have produced some of the most memorable live moments in entertainment history.
Oscar campaigns often include screenings, interviews, festival buzz, and trade-paper advertising aimed at Academy voters.
Craft categories like editing, cinematography, costume design, and production design are often where film lovers discover how much invisible work shapes a movie.
Winning an Oscar is considered one of the highest honors in the film industry, but many legendary artists were never awarded competitively.
The Academy also presents honorary awards recognizing lifetime achievement and extraordinary contributions to cinema.
The Oscars were first televised in 1953, helping turn the ceremony into a major annual media event.

It Happened One Night, One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest, and The Silence of the Lambs are the only three films to win the Oscars' 'Big Five' categories: picture, director, actor, actress, and screenplay.
Hattie McDaniel became the first Black person to win an Academy Award for her supporting role in Gone with the Wind.
Kathryn Bigelow became the first woman to win Best Director for The Hurt Locker at the 2010 Oscars.
Chloé Zhao became the second woman and the first woman of color to win Best Director, for Nomadland.
Daniel Day-Lewis holds the record for the most Best Actor wins, with three Oscars.
Frances McDormand has won three Best Actress Oscars, putting her among the most awarded performers in Academy history.
Edith Head won eight Academy Awards for costume design, more than any other woman in Oscar history.
John Williams has earned more Oscar nominations than any living person, thanks to scores for films like Star Wars, Jaws, E.T., and Schindler's List.
Gone with the Wind was the first color film to win Best Picture.
Midnight Cowboy remains the only X-rated film ever to win Best Picture.
The Godfather Part II became the first sequel to win Best Picture.
Marlee Matlin won Best Actress for Children of a Lesser God and remains the only deaf performer to win an acting Oscar.
Tatum O'Neal is the youngest competitive acting winner in Oscar history, winning at age 10 for Paper Moon.
Anthony Hopkins became the oldest Best Actor winner when he won for The Father.
In 1969, the Best Actress category ended in a rare tie between Katharine Hepburn and Barbra Streisand.
The 2017 Best Picture announcement mix-up briefly named La La Land the winner before Moonlight was correctly announced.
Everything Everywhere All at Once won seven Oscars, including Best Picture, at the 2023 ceremony.
CODA became the first film with a predominantly deaf cast to win Best Picture.
The Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award is one of the Academy's most prestigious honorary honors for creative producers.
The Jean Hersholt Humanitarian Award recognizes individuals in the film industry whose humanitarian efforts bring credit to the industry.
Best Original Song winners have ranged from classic Hollywood ballads to Bond themes, Disney anthems, and modern pop collaborations.
The Academy Award trophy cannot legally be sold on the open market without first being offered back to the Academy for one dollar.
Oscar nominations are often announced several weeks before the ceremony, creating a major second wave of attention for nominated films.
For many movie fans, the Oscars serve as both an awards show and a yearly snapshot of how Hollywood sees itself.
The Oscar statuette was designed by Cedric Gibbons and sculpted by George Stanley.
Wings was the very first film to win what later became known as Best Picture at the 1929 ceremony.
The Artist became the first mostly silent film to win Best Picture since Wings.
All About Eve, Titanic, and La La Land share the record for the most Oscar nominations received by a single film, with 14 each.
The Best Actress category has occasionally produced historic ties, including the famous 1969 tie between Katharine Hepburn and Barbra Streisand.
Heath Ledger won a posthumous Oscar for Best Supporting Actor for his performance as the Joker in The Dark Knight.
Joaquin Phoenix won Best Actor for Joker, making comic-book performances a growing part of Oscar history.
Spirited Away was the first hand-drawn and non-English-language film to win Best Animated Feature.
Beauty and the Beast was the first animated film ever nominated for Best Picture.
The Best Animated Feature category helped bring far more attention to animation as a serious awards contender after it was introduced.
Moonlight became the first LGBTQ-themed film to win Best Picture.
Parasite's Best Picture win was a landmark because it showed a non-English-language film could win Hollywood's top prize.
The Oscars are voted on in rounds, with branch members nominating within their own crafts before the full membership votes on Best Picture.
The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences was founded in 1927, two years before the first awards ceremony.
Ennio Morricone received a long-overdue competitive Oscar for The Hateful Eight after decades of iconic film scoring.
The 'In Memoriam' segment has become one of the most emotional recurring traditions of the Academy Awards broadcast.
Some of the biggest Oscar debates happen when critically beloved performances or films are shut out entirely, proving the awards never satisfy everyone.
Best Picture is the Academy Awards category most closely associated with overall film prestige.
Winning Best Picture can permanently change how a movie is remembered and marketed.
Best Picture is one of the standout topics people mention when discussing Academy Awards.
Any overview of Academy Awards usually includes Best Picture.
Best Picture helped define the legacy and reputation of Academy Awards.
